perturbation.original_text: Buyer and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Materials solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction with Owner. Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives who need to know that information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with Owner and who agree to keep that information confidential. Buyer shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer and its Representatives may use the Confidential Materials for evaluating a possible transaction with Owner. Buyer shall endeavor to keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives who need to know that information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with Owner and who agree to keep that information confidential. Buyer shall not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated 'Buyer and its Representatives *shall* use the Confidential Materials solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction with Owner' and 'Buyer *shall* keep the Confidential Materials confidential'. By changing 'shall' to 'may' and 'endeavor', the obligation on the Buyer is weakened. Additionally, the original text states that 'Buyer shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.' This has been negated in the changed text to 'Buyer shall not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.' This contradicts the intentions of paragraph 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Materials. Buyer and its Representatives may use the Confidential Materials for evaluating a possible transaction with Owner. Buyer shall endeavor to keep the Confidential Materials confidential and shall not disclose any of the Confidential Materials in any manner whatsoever, provided, however, that (i) Buyer may make any disclosure of information contained in the Confidential Materials to which Owner gives its prior written consent; and (ii) any information contained in the Confidential Materials may be disclosed to Buyer’s Representatives who need to know that information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with Owner and who agree to keep that information confidential. Buyer shall not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text clearly states 'Buyer shall keep the Confidential Materials confidential' and 'Buyer shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives'. The changed text weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality by using 'endeavor' instead of 'shall' and removes the Buyer's responsibility for breaches by its Representatives. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, solidifying that there is in-text contradiction of obligations based on the perturbation explanation. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because the change causes inconsistency and makes the enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Except as set forth herein, Buyer expressly acknowledges that the Confidential Materials are being provided solely as a courtesy and without any representation or warranty of any kind. As part of Buyer’s agreement to accept and use the Confidential Materials and Audit as such use is defined herein, and as consideration for the release of the Confidential Materials by Owner, BUYER HEREBY WAIVES ANY AND ALL ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL RIGHTS, CLAIMS, CAUSES OF ACTION OR DEMAND BUYER MIGHT HAVE REGARDING ANY FORM OF WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, OF ANY KIND OR TYPE, RELATING TO THE CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer expressly acknowledges that the Confidential Materials are being provided with a limited warranty of accuracy, but without any representation of completeness. As part of Buyer’s agreement to accept and use the Confidential Materials and Audit as such use is defined herein, and as consideration for the release of the Confidential Materials by Owner, BUYER HEREBY WAIVES ANY AND ALL ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL RIGHTS, CLAIMS, CAUSES OF ACTION OR DEMAND BUYER MIGHT HAVE REGARDING ANY FORM OF WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, OF ANY KIND OR TYPE, RELATING TO THE COMPLETENESS OF THE CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, section 5 disclaims all warranties related to the Confidential Materials. By changing the text to state that the Confidential Materials are being provided with a limited warranty of accuracy, but without any representation of completeness, and that the Buyer waives all claims related to the completeness of the Confidential Materials, creates an in-text contradiction within section 5. A limited warranty is now provided but then waived.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Release and Waiver of Warranties and Claims. Buyer expressly acknowledges that the Confidential Materials are being provided with a limited warranty of accuracy, but without any representation of completeness. As part of Buyer’s agreement to accept and use the Confidential Materials and Audit as such use is defined herein, and as consideration for the release of the Confidential Materials by Owner, BUYER HEREBY WAIVES ANY AND ALL ACTUAL OR POTENTIAL RIGHTS, CLAIMS, CAUSES OF ACTION OR DEMAND BUYER MIGHT HAVE REGARDING ANY FORM OF WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, OF ANY KIND OR TYPE, RELATING TO THE COMPLETENESS OF THE CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS.
perturbation.justification: The original text disclaims all warranties. The changed text introduces a limited warranty of accuracy while waiving claims related to completeness. This creates a direct contradiction because it initially provides a limited warranty and subsequently attempts to waive rights related to a specific aspect of that very warranty (completeness), but the contradiction occurs because the initial section now states there IS a limited warranty, which contradicts the waiver. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case) highlights the in-text contradiction. A reasonable reader would immediately recognize the sections as incompatible because it grants and denies warranty rights in the same passage.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer shall at all times conduct its due diligence review, inspections and examinations in a manner so as to not cause damage, loss, cost or expense to Owner, the Property or the tenants of the Property and so as to not interfere with or disturb any tenant at the Property, and Buyer will indemnify, protect, defend, and hold Owner and the Property harmless from and against any such breach of this Agreement. Without limitation on the foregoing, in no event shall Buyer contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent. Owner shall have the right, at its option, to cause a representative of Owner to be present at all inspections, reviews and examinations conducted hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer may conduct its due diligence review, inspections and examinations in a manner that minimizes damage, loss, cost or expense to Owner, the Property or the tenants of the Property and may interfere or disturb any tenant at the Property, and Buyer will attempt to indemnify, protect, defend, and hold Owner and the Property harmless from and against any such breach of this Agreement. Without limitation on the foregoing, Buyer may contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent. Owner shall not have the right, at its option, to cause a representative of Owner to be present at all inspections, reviews and examinations conducted hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: In the original agreement, the Buyer *shall* conduct due diligence without causing damage and *shall not* contact tenants without consent. This is a strict obligation. The modified text weakens this obligation by stating Buyer *may* conduct due diligence in a manner that minimizes damage, allows for interference with tenants, and allowing contact with tenants without consent. Furthermore, it takes away Owner's right to have a representative at inspections. The changes create an internal contradiction within the paragraph by changing the Buyer's responsibilites while still stating the agreement will be honored.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Due Diligence. Buyer may conduct its due diligence review, inspections and examinations in a manner that minimizes damage, loss, cost or expense to Owner, the Property or the tenants of the Property and may interfere or disturb any tenant at the Property, and Buyer will attempt to indemnify, protect, defend, and hold Owner and the Property harmless from and against any such breach of this Agreement. Without limitation on the foregoing, Buyer may contact any tenant of the Property without Owner’s express written consent. Owner shall not have the right, at its option, to cause a representative of Owner to be present at all inspections, reviews and examinations conducted hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains strict obligations for the Buyer regarding due diligence, tenant interaction, and Owner's access during inspections. The changed text weakens these obligations, allowing for interference with tenants, contact without consent, and denying Owner's representative access. Because the contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, it proves that there is a contradiction. The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement's intent, thus creating a direct contradiction. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities' - 'In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could not be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Buyer shall reimburse Owner for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney’s fees, incurred by Owner if Owner successfully enforces the obligations of Buyer and its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, may not be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Buyer will consider reimbursing Owner for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney’s fees, incurred by Owner if Owner successfully enforces the obligations of Buyer and its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the contract specifies that the Owner *shall* be entitled to an injunction and specific performance, and the Buyer *shall* reimburse costs. The modified text uses 'could be made whole by monetary damages', 'may not be entitled to an injunction', and 'will consider reimbursing', weakening the Owner's remedies and creating uncertainty. This creates a direct contradiction in section 7 where the agreement originally provided clear remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies. Buyer acknowledges that if this Agreement is breached, Owner could be made whole by monetary damages. Accordingly, Owner, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled by law or in equity, may not be entitled to an injunction to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to an order compelling specific performance of this Agreement. Buyer will consider reimbursing Owner for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorney’s fees, incurred by Owner if Owner successfully enforces the obligations of Buyer and its Representatives hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Owner 'shall' be entitled to an injunction and specific performance, and the Buyer 'shall' reimburse costs. The changed text weakens these obligations by using 'could be made whole by monetary damages', 'may not be entitled to an injunction', and 'will consider reimbursing'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This means the original strong remedies are now weakened in the changed text, directly contradicting the original intent of ensuring specific remedies. This creates a direct contradiction as it introduces uncertainty and weakens the owner's ability to enforce the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: All of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer. Buyer may not assign this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement are non-binding and do not inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer. Buyer may assign this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 9(a) originally stated that the agreement is binding and the Buyer cannot assign the agreement. The updated text directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement is *not* binding and that the Buyer *may* assign the agreement. This creates a contradiction for paragraph 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous a) Assignment; Binding Effect. All of the covenants, conditions and obligations contained in this Agreement are non-binding and do not inure to the benefit of the respective successors, permitted assigns and designees of Owner, and the successors of Buyer. Buyer may assign this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement is 'non-binding' and the Buyer 'may assign' the agreement. The contradicted text mirrors this change, stating the same. However, the original text stated the agreement 'shall be binding' and 'Buyer may not assign this agreement'. Because the changed text directly negates the original text's conditions while the contradicted text agrees with the changed text, there is a strong contradiction.
